title: What Can You Do?
titleJa: なにができますか
description: Practice the potential form by interviewing a partner about abilities and skills.
canDo: Can ask and answer questions about what you can and cannot do using the potential form.
sampleDialogue: 日本語で手紙が書けますか。
practicePrompts: Ask your partner three things they can do in Japanese.
Tell your partner about something you could not do before but can do now.
Discuss which skills you both want to be able to do in the future.

title: Childhood Abilities
titleJa: こどものころ できたこと
description: Talk about what you could and couldn't do as a child using the potential form in past tense.
canDo: Can compare past and present abilities using the potential form in both present and past tense.
sampleDialogue: 子供のころ、泳げましたか。いいえ、全然泳げませんでした。
practicePrompts: Ask your partner two things they could do as a child that they can still do now.
Share something you couldn't do as a child but can do well today.
Talk about a skill you had as a child but have since lost, using できた and できなくなった.

title: Asking for and Offering Help
titleJa: たすけをたのむ
description: Practice giving and receiving directions and favors using てあげる, てもらう, and てくれる.
canDo: Can describe favors given and received, and politely ask someone for help.
sampleDialogue: すみません、駅までの道を教えてもらえませんか。
practicePrompts: Ask your partner to explain how to get to the nearest station.
Describe something a friend did for you recently using てくれる.
Offer to help your partner with something using てあげる.

title: Thanking Someone for a Favour
titleJa: おれいをいう
description: Express gratitude and describe in detail what someone did for you using てくれる and てもらう.
canDo: Can express gratitude for a favour and explain what was done for you using てくれる and てもらう.
sampleDialogue: 山田さんが荷物を運んでくれたので、本当に助かりました。
practicePrompts: Tell your partner about a time a friend or family member helped you, using てくれました.
Describe what you asked someone to do for you using てもらいました.
Thank your partner for an imaginary favour they did, explaining why it was so helpful.

title: Weekend Plans
titleJa: しゅうまつのよてい
description: Practice the volitional form to discuss plans and intentions with a partner.
canDo: Can discuss future plans and intentions using the volitional form and ようと思う.
sampleDialogue: 週末は何をしようと思っていますか。
practicePrompts: Ask your partner what they are planning to do this weekend.
Share three things you intend to do next month using ようと思う.
Suggest doing something together using the volitional form.

title: Sharing a Resolution
titleJa: けっしんをはなす
description: Explain a decision you made and the reasoning behind it using ようと思った and ことにした.
canDo: Can explain a decision or resolution and give reasons for it using ようと思った and ことにした.
sampleDialogue: 健康のために、毎朝走ることにしました。
practicePrompts: Share a recent decision you made using ことにしました and explain why.
Tell your partner about something you decided to start or stop doing this year.
Ask your partner if they have made any resolutions recently and react to their answer.

title: Wishes and Requests
titleJa: おねがいとのぞみ
description: Practice expressing wishes and reporting what others said using てほしい and indirect quotation.
canDo: Can express what you want someone to do and report what someone else said.
sampleDialogue: ルームメートにもっと静かにしてほしいんですが。
practicePrompts: Tell your partner two things you want your family to do using てほしい.
Report what your teacher said in class today using indirect quotation.
Discuss something you wish your friend would stop doing.

title: Passing on a Message
titleJa: つたごとをつたえる
description: Relay a request or piece of news from a third person using indirect speech with と言っていました and てほしいと言っていました.
canDo: Can relay a request or message from a third person using indirect speech patterns.
sampleDialogue: 先生が明日レポートを出してほしいと言っていましたよ。
practicePrompts: Relay a message from an absent friend to your partner using と言っていました.
Pass on a request from a teacher or boss using てほしいと言っていました.
Tell your partner some news you heard from someone else, reporting their exact words.

title: What Would You Do If...?
titleJa: もし...だったら
description: Practice conditional expressions to discuss hypothetical and real-life situations.
canDo: Can discuss hypothetical situations and their outcomes using たら and もし.
sampleDialogue: もし百万円あったら、何をしますか。
practicePrompts: Ask your partner what they would do if they had a million yen.
Discuss what you will do if the weather is nice this weekend.
Share what would happen if you could live in any country.

title: Planning for an Emergency
titleJa: きんきゅうじたいのそなえ
description: Discuss what you would do in a hypothetical bad situation such as a natural disaster or sudden illness using もし and たら.
canDo: Can discuss emergency plans and hypothetical bad situations using もし and たら conditionals.
sampleDialogue: もし地震が起きたら、まず何をしますか。
practicePrompts: Discuss what you would do first if there were a big earthquake using もし〜たら.
Ask your partner what they would do if they suddenly felt very sick while traveling abroad.
Compare your emergency plans and decide together which actions to take first.

title: Describing How Things Are
titleJa: ものの ようす
description: Practice transitive and intransitive verbs to describe the state of a room or situation.
canDo: Can describe the current state of things using transitive and intransitive verb pairs.
sampleDialogue: 窓が開いていますよ。だれが開けたんですか。
practicePrompts: Describe five things you notice about the state of a messy room.
Ask your partner who did each action using transitive verbs.
Explain what has changed in your room since this morning.

title: Reporting an Accident or Mishap
titleJa: じこやしっぱいをはなす
description: Describe what broke, spilled, or got left behind using intransitive verbs and the ている state form.
canDo: Can report an accident or mishap by describing the resulting state using intransitive verbs and ている.
sampleDialogue: コーヒーがこぼれて、ノートが濡れています。
practicePrompts: Describe a small accident you had recently — what happened and what state things are in now.
Report to your partner that something in the room is broken or out of place using intransitive verbs.
React to your partner's mishap story by asking follow-up questions about the current state of things.

title: Speaking Politely at Work
titleJa: しごとでの ていねいな ことば
description: Practice honorific and humble speech forms in workplace and formal scenarios.
canDo: Can use honorific and humble forms appropriately when speaking to superiors or customers.
sampleDialogue: 部長、明日の会議にいらっしゃいますか。
practicePrompts: Greet your manager and ask about their schedule using honorific forms.
Describe what you did at work today using humble forms.
Role-play answering the phone at an office and transferring a call politely.

title: Calling a Company
titleJa: かいしゃにでんわをかける
description: Practise making a formal phone call to a company to inquire about a product or service using keigo.
canDo: Can make a polite phone inquiry to a company using honorific and humble speech forms.
sampleDialogue: お世話になっております。商品についてお伺いしたいのですが、よろしいでしょうか。
practicePrompts: Call a company and introduce yourself politely, explaining why you are calling.
Ask about the price and availability of a product using formal honorific expressions.
Close the call by thanking the representative and summarising what was decided.

title: Giving Advice with Reasons
titleJa: りゆうを つけて アドバイス
description: Practice giving reasons and conditional advice using なら and other expressions.
canDo: Can give advice with reasons and express conditions using なら.
sampleDialogue: 日本に行くなら、京都がおすすめですよ。
practicePrompts: Your partner wants to visit Japan. Give advice using なら.
Recommend a restaurant, giving reasons why it is good.
Ask for and give advice about choosing a new phone or laptop.

title: Adapting Plans to the Situation
titleJa: じょうきょうにあわせてけいかくをかえる
description: Use なら to adjust plans based on weather, schedules, or other changing circumstances.
canDo: Can use なら to offer alternative plans or suggestions based on a given situation or condition.
sampleDialogue: 雨なら、外でのピクニックはやめて、映画を見に行きましょう。
practicePrompts: Your weekend plans might change because of rain. Suggest alternatives using なら.
Your partner says they are tired. Propose a different activity suited to their condition using なら.
Discuss two possible scenarios for an upcoming trip and what you would do in each case.

title: Things That Happened to Me
titleJa: あった できごと
description: Practice the passive form to narrate experiences and things that happened to you.
canDo: Can describe experiences using the passive form, including inconvenient situations.
sampleDialogue: 電車の中で足を踏まれました。
practicePrompts: Tell your partner about an annoying experience using the passive form.
Ask your partner if they have ever been praised or scolded by a teacher.
Describe a time when you were surprised by something someone did.

title: A Story from the Recipient's View
titleJa: うけてがわからのはなし
description: Retell an event from the perspective of the person who was acted on, using the passive form to express how an action felt.
canDo: Can retell an event from the recipient's perspective using the passive form to convey emotion and impact.
sampleDialogue: 急に友達に秘密を話されて、とても困りました。
practicePrompts: Retell a story you know from the point of view of the person who had something done to them.
Describe how you felt when someone praised, criticised, or surprised you, using the passive form.
Ask your partner to tell a story from the recipient's perspective and ask how they felt about it.

title: Making and Letting Others Do Things
titleJa: させたり させてもらったり
description: Practice the causative form to discuss making or letting someone do something.
canDo: Can describe situations where someone made or let another person do something using the causative form.
sampleDialogue: 子供の時、母に毎日ピアノを練習させられました。
practicePrompts: Talk about things your parents made you do as a child.
Ask your partner what their boss lets them do at work.
Discuss whether teachers should let students use phones in class.

title: Being Made to Do Something Unwanted
titleJa: いやなことをさせられた
description: Express frustration about being forced into something using the causative-passive form.
canDo: Can describe being made to do something against one's will using the causative-passive form and express how it felt.
sampleDialogue: 上司にずっと残業させられて、本当につかれました。
practicePrompts: Tell your partner about something you were made to do that you really did not want to do, using させられました.
Describe a childhood memory where you were forced to do something by a parent or teacher.
Compare experiences with your partner — who was made to do the most unwanted thing?

title: Talking About Everything
titleJa: いろいろな はなし
description: Review pair work combining multiple grammar points from lessons 13-22 in free conversation.
canDo: Can hold a natural conversation combining potential, passive, causative, conditional, and honorific forms.
sampleDialogue: 日本に住んだら、何ができるようになりたいですか。
practicePrompts: Discuss your future goals using potential and volitional forms together.
Tell a story about a memorable experience using passive and causative forms.
Give your partner advice about a problem using conditionals and reasons.

title: Reviewing a Restaurant or Experience
titleJa: レストランやたいけんのレビュー
description: Give a nuanced review of a restaurant or activity by combining multiple grammar forms learned across lessons 13-22.
canDo: Can give a balanced, detailed review using potential, passive, conditional, and giving/receiving forms together.
sampleDialogue: 料理はとても食べやすくて、店員さんにも親切にしてもらいました。また行けたら、行きたいです。
practicePrompts: Review a restaurant you have been to, using てもらう to describe good service and potential form for what you could enjoy.
Describe an activity or trip using passive form for things that happened to you and conditionals for what you would do differently.
Ask your partner for their review of somewhere they visited and respond with follow-up questions using a variety of grammar forms.
